title: Kundalini and the Inner Child: Healing What Was Broken Before the Awakening
meta_description: You're here, aren't you? Probably staring at your screen in the dead of night, heart pounding, mind racing. Maybe you've felt that fire, that weird energy,
body:
Kundalini and the Inner Child: Healing What Was Broken Before the Awakening

You're here, aren't you? Probably staring at your screen in the dead of night, heart pounding, mind racing. Maybe you've felt that fire, that weird energy, the terrifying sensations. Or maybe you're just starting to connect the dots, wondering if what you're experiencing is... that thing. Kundalini. And you're scared. You feel alone. I know that feeling. I've been there. The cold sweat, the panic attacks that come out of nowhere, the feeling like your body's betraying you, your mind's unraveling. It's a special kind of hell, isn't it? Especially when everyone around you just doesn't get it. They tell you to meditate more, to just 'breathe,' or worse - they dismiss it as anxiety or some psychological breakdown. And yeah, it can look like that. But here's the thing: it's often more. Much more.

What they don't tell you, what isn't in most of the fluffy spiritual books, is that Kundalini isn't just about bliss and enlightenment. It's a damn wrecking ball. And it doesn't just clear out the junk you picked up last week. It goes for the foundation. It goes for the stuff that was broken long before you even knew what Kundalini was. It goes for your inner child. Right? You feel it. That deep, ancient pain that suddenly comes roaring to the surface, demanding to be seen. It's not just the energy moving; it's the energy clearing out decades of stored trauma, neglect, and unresolved hurt. It's brutal. It's messy. And you're not going crazy. You're just hitting the motherlode of your unhealed past.

I remember being in the thick of it. The constant tremors, the bizarre visions, the overwhelming grief that would just wash over me for no apparent reason. I'd curl up in a ball, sobbing, feeling like a little kid who'd been abandoned. And in those moments, I knew it wasn't just my adult self freaking out. It was something deeper. Something that had been buried for so long, and now this raw, primal energy was digging it up. It's like your system knows it can't truly integrate higher consciousness until it cleans house. And sometimes, cleaning house means tearing down walls you didn't even know were there. It means facing the parts of you that got hurt, the parts that got silenced, the parts that never felt safe. That's your inner child screaming for attention. And Kundalini, bless its relentless heart, is giving it a megaphone. You know what I mean?

The Uninvited Guest: Kundalini Exposing Old Wounds

Look, when Kundalini wakes up, it's not asking for an invitation. It just shows up, kicks down the door, and starts rummaging through your attic. And what's in that attic? All the stuff you thought you'd dealt with. All the stuff you buried deep down because it was too painful to look at. The shame, the fear, the feelings of not being enough, of not being loved. That's the inner child stuff. That's the little you who didn't get what they needed, who felt alone, who was criticized, or worse, ignored. And now, with this immense energy flowing through you, all those old wounds are getting blasted wide open.

You might experience intense emotional flashbacks. Suddenly, you're not just sad; you're feeling the exact sadness you felt when you were five and your parents were fighting. You're not just anxious; you're feeling the terror of being left alone in the dark. It's vivid. It's real. And it's happening in your adult body, which can make it feel even more disorienting. You're a grown-ass person, but you're having the emotional meltdowns of a toddler. It's humiliating, isolating, and utterly terrifying. I've been there, screaming into a pillow because the pain was so overwhelming, feeling like I was regressing instead of evolving. It makes you question everything, doesn't it? You thought you were on a spiritual path, and now you're just a mess.

Here's the thing: Kundalini doesn't care about your spiritual ego. It cares about wholeness. And you can't be whole if you've got gaping wounds from childhood festering beneath the surface. This energy, it's intelligent. It knows exactly where the blockages are, where the energy flow is constricted because of old hurts. And it will push and push and push until those blockages clear. Sometimes that push feels like a gentle nudge. More often, especially when there's deep trauma, it feels like an explosion. It feels like you're shattering. And in a way, you are. You're shattering the old self, the wounded self, to make way for something new.

The Body Remembers Everything

One of the most intense aspects of this process is how your body reacts. You might get weird physical sensations that doctors can't explain. Chronic pain that flares up, strange tingling, burning, vibrations that feel like they're going to tear you apart. And often, these physical symptoms are directly linked to where that childhood trauma is stored in your body. Your body isn't just a vehicle; it's a living archive of every single experience you've ever had, especially the traumatic ones. That knot in your stomach? Could be years of fear and anxiety. That tension in your jaw? Could be all the times you couldn't speak your truth as a kid. The tightness in your chest? Could be grief and heartbreak that never got expressed.

When Kundalini starts moving, it's literally shaking these stored memories loose from your cells, from your fascia, from your nervous system. It's a biological process as much as it is a spiritual one. It's why you can feel so exhausted, so drained, even when you haven't done anything physically demanding. Your body is doing intense, deep-level processing. It's why you might crave certain foods, or suddenly become sensitive to things you never were before. Your system is trying to rebalance, to purge, to heal. It's not just "energy moving." It's a deep physiological and psychological upheaval. You're literally releasing decades of emotional baggage from your physical form. It's an intense detox, and you're feeling every single pang of it.

I've found that reading books like The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link) can help you understand the physical manifestations. It's not just in your head. Other people have felt this. You're not alone in these bizarre sensations. Knowing that can be a lifesaver when you're convinced you're losing it.

Meeting Your Inner Child in the Fire

So, what do you do when your inner child is screaming, and Kundalini is fanning the flames? You don't ignore it. You can't. This isn't a "power through it" situation. This is a "stop everything and listen" situation. This is where the real work begins. You have to become the loving, attentive, safe parent you needed back then, for yourself, right now. It's hard. Especially when you're feeling overwhelmed, exhausted, and frankly, furious that this is all happening.

You need to create a space of safety for that wounded part of you. Imagine that little you. What did they need? What were they deprived of? Was it comfort? Validation? A voice? Unconditional love? Now, you have to give that to yourself. It's not about intellectualizing it. It's about feeling it. It's about allowing yourself to feel the pain, the anger, the sadness, without judgment. Just let it be there. Let it move through you. Because that's what Kundalini wants. It wants movement. It wants flow. And stuck emotions are the biggest blockages.

This isn't about blaming your parents or whoever hurt you. It's about acknowledging the wound within you and taking responsibility for its healing. It's about saying, "Okay, little one, I see you. I hear you. And I'm here for you now. I won't abandon you." This inner dialogue, this internal parenting, is crucial. It's how you integrate those fragmented parts of yourself back into a whole. It's how you soothe the terrified child who's been running the show from the shadows for decades. Does that land?

Practical Steps for Inner Child Healing During Awakening

When you're in the throes of a Kundalini awakening, just surviving can feel like a full-time job. But you can't neglect the inner child work. It's not an optional extra; it's central to navigating this process without completely losing your mind. Here are some things I found helpful, and things I wish I'd done sooner:

  • Acknowledge and Validate: When intense emotions hit, instead of pushing them away, try saying, "I see you, little one. I know you're hurting. It's okay to feel this." Give yourself permission to feel whatever comes up. No judgment.
  • Journaling: Get it all out. Write letters to your inner child, or from your inner child to you. Let the raw emotions flow onto the page. Don't censor yourself. This is for you.
  • Creative Expression: If words don't come easily, try drawing, painting, or even just scribbling. Sometimes the inner child communicates best through non-verbal means.
  • Comfort and Nurturing: What would comfort a scared child? A warm blanket? A favorite stuffed animal? Gentle music? A warm bath? Give yourself those things. Treat yourself with the utmost kindness and gentleness.
  • Grounding Practices: When the energy is too much, and the emotions are overwhelming, ground yourself. Walk barefoot on the earth, hug a tree, eat nourishing food, spend time in nature. Anything that connects you back to your physical body and the present moment. Tibetan Singing Bowl Set - Meditation & Sound Healing (paid link) can be really helpful for grounding and bringing you back to your body when your mind is racing.
  • Therapy: Honestly, if you can afford it, find a trauma-informed therapist. Someone who understands childhood trauma and ideally, someone who has some awareness of spiritual emergencies. They can provide a safe container and guidance that you just can't get on your own. You don't have to do this alone.
  • Connect with Others: Find communities or individuals who understand what you're going through. Online forums, support groups - just knowing you're not the only one can be incredibly validating. Books like When Spirit Leaps: Navigating the Process of Spiritual Awakening by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) and The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) can also make you feel less isolated.

Integration: The Long Road Home

Healing your inner child during a Kundalini awakening isn't a one-and-done deal. It's a process. It's an ongoing commitment. It's about integration. Kundalini brings everything up, but it doesn't do the integration for you. That's your job. That's where your conscious awareness, your compassion, and your willingness to sit with discomfort come in. You're basically re-parenting yourself, creating new neural pathways, and teaching your nervous system that it's finally safe to relax, to be seen, to be loved.

The goal isn't to get rid of the inner child. It's to heal them, to integrate them, so they're no longer running your life from a place of fear and pain. It's about bringing that wounded part of you into the light, acknowledging its existence, and offering it the love and acceptance it never received. When you do this, something incredible happens. The intensity of the Kundalini symptoms often lessens. The energy flows more smoothly. The terror gives way to a sense of peace, even joy. Because you've cleared out some of the biggest blockages. You've answered the call of your deepest, most vulnerable self.

I'm not gonna lie; it's hard work. It's probably the hardest work you'll ever do. There will be days you feel like you're losing the battle. Days you just want to curl up and disappear. But every time you show up for that little you inside, every time you offer kindness instead of criticism, every time you allow yourself to feel the pain without judgment, you're taking a step towards true healing. You're building a bridge between your adult self and your wounded child self, and that bridge is what allows Kundalini to truly do its work - not just to awaken, but to bring you to a state of complete, integrated wholeness.

You're not broken beyond repair. You're being remade. And yeah, it feels like everything's falling apart. But sometimes, things have to fall apart so something better can be built in their place. And you, my friend, are building something incredible. You're building a healed, whole, and genuinely awakened self. Keep going. You've got this. I know you do. I've seen it. I've lived it.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
